description_long: This is a regional jet airliner painted in the Delta Air Lines livery, featuring a long, slender fuselage with a pointed nose and a T-tail configuration. The aircraft has low-mounted, swept-back wings with small winglets at the tips, and two turbofan engines mounted at the rear of the fuselage. The landing gear is in a tricycle configuration, with the main gear under the wings and a nose gear at the front. The cockpit is located at the very front of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the pilots. The tail fin is painted in Delta's signature blue and red colors, while the main body is predominantly white with the airline's branding. The aircraft is designed for passenger transport and is enterable, with doors and windows along the fuselage.
description: A Delta Air Lines regional jet with a white fuselage, blue and red T-tail, low-mounted swept wings, and rear-mounted twin turbofan engines. The aircraft features a tricycle landing gear and a forward, enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the Delta livery, full fuselage, T-tail, and both engines clearly visible.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the twin rear-mounted engines, blue tail fin, and the full wingspan with visible winglets.
description_view_2: Opposite side view of the fuselage, showing the Delta branding, windows, and landing gear.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the pointed nose, cockpit windows, and the full span of the swept wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back wings with small upward winglets at the tips and slight dihedral angle.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and high-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and one nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows for pilot visibility.
